content.thinking:
Now I can see the issues:

1. The replica set is healthy - 1 PRIMARY (mongodb-replica-1) and 2 SECONDARY members. So it's working now.
2. The `monitoring` label is **missing** from all pods. The task says I need to restore the pod-template `monitoring` label to `enabled`.

The pods don't have `monitoring=enabled` label. I need to add this label to the StatefulSet pod template so it propagates to the pods.

Let me check the current StatefulSet spec to see if this label was there before and was removed.

content.text: The replica set is healthy (1 PRIMARY, 2 SECONDARYs), but pods are missing the `monitoring` label. I need to patch the StatefulSet pod template to add `monitoring: enabled`.

input.command: kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o yaml 2>&1

content.thinking:
I can see the issue now. The StatefulSet pod template labels only have `app: mongodb-replica` but are missing the `monitoring: enabled` label. The original `last-applied-configuration` annotation also shows it was missing from the beginning.

I need to patch the StatefulSet to add `monitoring: enabled` to the pod template labels. This will trigger a rolling update of the pods.

Let me patch the StatefulSet pod template to add the monitoring label.

content.text: The pod template is missing `monitoring: enabled`. I'll patch the StatefulSet to add it, which will trigger a rolling update.

input.description: Patch StatefulSet pod template to add monitoring=enabled label
